name: Unspecified open() Encoding
description: open() without explicit encoding may behave differently across platforms.
rationale: On Windows, open() defaults to the system ANSI code page (e.g. CP1252), while on Linux it uses UTF-8. Explicit encoding ensures consistent behavior.
noncompliant_code:
with open('data.txt') as f:
    content = f.read()
compliant_code:
with open('data.txt', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()
